authority: Official Firewire and Slater Designs model descriptions and governed Quivrr catalogue records

models.reason: Firewire positions the Boss Up as a versatile utility midlength with one-board-quiver range from weak surf to powerful overhead waves.
The Dominator 2.0 is explicitly designed as a broad-range everyday board with easy speed, paddle support and improved performance rails.
Although derived from the Dominator, the Pro is deliberately refined to compete with a conventional performance shortboard in better waves and on surf trips.
The DRK is a Stab in the Dark winning design built around refined FRK performance DNA for high-performance surfing in good waves.
The swallow-tail FRK remains a dedicated high-performance shortboard, with the tail variation changing release and hold rather than its primary family.
The FRK+ is a squash-tail HPSB tuned for faster rail transitions and broader everyday conditions without leaving the performance-shortboard lane.
Firewire explicitly calls it a high-performance groveller, combining hybrid paddle and catching with shortboard rail performance in weak and punchy surf.
Its compressed longboard outline, glide, paddle power and shortboard manoeuvrability make it a true crossover midlength.
Rob Machado describes the Groove as his highest-performance design, with increased rocker and pocket-focused shortboard surfing as its defining role.
The Machadocado combines explicit fish DNA, small-wave planing and shortboard performance in a modern performance-fish package.
Firewire describes the Mashup as the ultimate groveller, blending Seaside weak-wave speed with Spitfire vertical performance.
The Mindcraft is a precision-focused Tomo and Slater performance design using a minimalist outline and twin-derived speed without fish-first intent.
The Neutrino is a modern planing-hull performance hybrid built to generate speed in small waves while retaining broad everyday usability.
Firewire presents the Revo Max as a performance midlength and step-up twin in one, with the longer rail line and midlength platform remaining primary.
The S Boss is explicitly positioned as a universal, user-friendly performance shape for progression across varied conditions and skill levels.
The Sci-Fi 2.0 is a modern planing-hull performance shortboard designed for control, speed and serious wave range.
The Seaside is Rob Machado's higher-performance evolution of the Go Fish, retaining fish identity while adding tighter turns and more carving control.
The Seaside & Beyond is a stretched Seaside built around longer fish glide and flow, so Fish remains the primary family with midlength scale as the subtype.
The Singleton is a modern noserider developed with Taylor Jensen, with tip stability, nose concave and longboard manoeuvrability as its defining identity.
The Spaceship is explicitly a high-performance good-wave board designed for tube hold, rail turns and control in round, powerful wave faces.
The Special T is Firewire's traditional longboard and noserider, using soft rails, nose concave and added tail flip for classic trim and tip time.
Firewire explicitly identifies the Sweet Potato as its high-performance groveller for extremely weak waves.
The Hull Rider is an easy-paddling, stable all-round longboard with hull glide and noseriding support.
The Taylor Jensen Pro is his competition longboard, designed for vertical performance while retaining full longboard functionality.
The TJ Pro Mid is explicitly a fast-paddling performance midlength derived from Taylor Jensen's longboard design language.
The TJ Pro V is an all-conditions performance longboard tuned for average surf without sacrificing longboard manoeuvrability.
Firewire lists the Twinzer within its crossover and midlength range; its longer platform and twinzer performance make Mid Length the clearest public family.
The Gem is explicitly a one-board-quiver longboard balancing trim, turns and all-round versatility.
The Sunday is Rob Machado's relaxed midlength cruiser, designed around glide, ease and a broad weekend-wave range.
The Xtra Cado is explicitly the midlength evolution of the Machadocado, designed for glide, easier surfing and longer drawn-out turns.

review_notes: All thirty governed Firewire models have an explicit public family and detailed category.
Dominator Pro, DRK, Spaceship and Mindcraft were moved or confirmed in Performance Shortboard because their primary manufacturer intent is high-performance surfing rather than everyday versatility.
Great White Twin, Mashup and Sweet Potato are governed as Grovellers because weak-wave speed generation is their defining role.
Taylor Jensen Twinzer is governed as a performance midlength rather than Step Up because Firewire positions it in the crossover and midlength range.
Seaside & Beyond remains in Fish as a midlength performance fish because the stretched fish identity is stronger than a generic midlength label.
Construction and lamination variants do not change the underlying family of the model.
